title: Battle of Crete (Operation Mercury)
summary: Operation Mercury (Unternehmen Merkur) was the German airborne invasion of Crete — the world's first primarily airborne conquest of a major island. Despite catastrophic initial losses, the Fallschirmjäger secured a foothold at Maleme airfield and the Allies were forced to evacuate.

axis.strength: 22,000 paratroopers and mountain troops, 500 transport aircraft

allied.strength: 42,640 Allied troops (British, Australian, New Zealand, Greek)

decisive_moment: The capture of Maleme airfield on 21 May by a single German battalion, after the New Zealand commander ordered a controversial withdrawal. With Maleme secure, Germany could fly in mountain troops faster than Allied forces could counterattack.
outcome: German victory; Allies evacuated. However, Fallschirmjäger casualties were so severe — ~6,500 killed or wounded out of 22,000 — that Hitler refused to sanction further large-scale airborne operations. Student later called Crete 'the graveyard of the German paratroopers.'
